tips: The best time to ask for a major gift is when the donor feels emotionally connected to your cause.
Always send a handwritten thank you note within 48 hours of receiving a gift.
Donors give to people, not organisations. Make sure your fundraiser is the face of the relationship.
Never ask for a specific amount in your first major gift conversation. Listen first.
A lapsed donor who gave three or more times is worth more effort than a brand new prospect.
Stewardship is not a post-gift activity. It starts the moment someone makes their first donation.
The most powerful words in fundraising are: tell me more about why this cause matters to you.
Regular giving is the foundation of a healthy fundraising program. Prioritise acquisition and retention equally.
Impact reporting is not optional. Every major donor deserves to know what their gift achieved.
A donor who volunteers is twice as likely to upgrade their giving. Engage them beyond the cheque.
Never assume a donor's capacity. Let them surprise you with their generosity.
The ask is only 10% of the major gift process. The other 90% is relationship building.
Peer-to-peer fundraising works because people trust their friends more than organisations.
A bequest conversation is not about death. It is about legacy and what matters most in life.
Your thank you call is more important than your ask call. Make it personal and specific.
Donors who feel ignored will lapse. A simple check-in call every quarter makes a huge difference.
The best major gift officers are great listeners, not great talkers.
Every complaint from a donor is a gift. It tells you exactly what to fix.
Corporate partnerships work best when both parties can articulate a shared value.
Never send a generic appeal to your top 20 donors. They deserve a personal conversation.
A mid-level giving program bridges the gap between mass fundraising and major gifts.
Gratitude must be proportional to the gift. A $10,000 donor should not get the same letter as a $50 donor.
The most successful fundraisers ask themselves: would I give to this appeal?
Retention is cheaper than acquisition. Invest in your existing donors first.
A strong case for support answers one question: why should I give to you instead of someone else?
Recurring donors have three times the lifetime value of one-off donors.
The best time to upgrade a donor is immediately after a positive impact story.
Prospecting without qualifying is wasted effort. Know your donor before you meet them.
Events are cultivation tools, not fundraising tools. Use them to deepen relationships.
A donor survey is one of the best investments you can make in your fundraising program.
Never negotiate a donor down from their intended gift. Let them be as generous as they want.
Digital fundraising works best when it feels personal, not broadcast.
Your board members are your best major gift prospects. Engage them as donors first.
A legacy pledge is not a gift you will ever see. Steward it as if it were cash in the bank.
The phone is still the most effective fundraising tool ever invented. Use it.
Donors who give in memory of someone have deep emotional attachment. Handle with care.
A matching gift campaign can double your revenue without doubling your effort.
Transparency builds trust. Share your financials openly with your major donors.
The best fundraising copy speaks to one person, not a crowd.
Never stop thanking. There is no such thing as too much genuine gratitude.
A gift in a will can be worth more than a lifetime of annual giving combined.

quotes.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care.
quotes.author: Theodore Roosevelt

quotes.text: We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: No one has ever become poor by giving.
quotes.author: Anne Frank

quotes.text: The meaning of life is to find your gift. The purpose of life is to give it away.
quotes.author: Pablo Picasso

quotes.text: Generosity is not giving me that which I need more than you do, but it is giving me that which you need more than I do.
quotes.author: Kahlil Gibran

quotes.text: You have not lived today until you have done something for someone who can never repay you.
quotes.author: John Bunyan

quotes.text: The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: Kindness is the language which the deaf can hear and the blind can see.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: We rise by lifting others.
quotes.author: Robert Ingersoll

quotes.text: How wonderful that nobody need wait a single moment before starting to improve the world.
quotes.author: Anne Frank

quotes.text: Alone we can do so little. Together we can do so much.
quotes.author: Helen Keller

quotes.text: The smallest act of kindness is worth more than the grandest intention.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: To give without any reward, or any notice, has a special quality of its own.
quotes.author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

quotes.text: Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present.
quotes.author: Albert Camus

quotes.text: If you want happiness for a lifetime, help somebody.
quotes.author: Chinese Proverb

quotes.text: The world is changed by your example, not by your opinion.
quotes.author: Paulo Coelho

quotes.text: Life's most persistent and urgent question is: what are you doing for others?
quotes.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

quotes.text: It's not how much we give but how much love we put into giving.
quotes.author: Mother Teresa

quotes.text: Do your little bit of good where you are. It's those little bits of good put together that overwhelm the world.
quotes.author: Desmond Tutu

quotes.text: The purpose of human life is to serve and to show compassion and the will to help others.
quotes.author: Albert Schweitzer

quotes.text: We cannot hold a torch to light another's path without brightening our own.
quotes.author: Ben Sweetland

quotes.text: Giving is not just about making a donation. It is about making a difference.
quotes.author: Kathy Calvin

quotes.text: Those who are happiest are those who do the most for others.
quotes.author: Booker T. Washington

quotes.text: One of the greatest gifts you can give is your time.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A candle loses nothing by lighting another candle.
quotes.author: James Keller

quotes.text: The heart that gives, gathers.
quotes.author: Tao Te Ching

quotes.text: Think of giving not as a duty but as a privilege.
quotes.author: John D. Rockefeller Jr.

quotes.text: We make a living by what we get. We make a life by what we give.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: Thousands of candles can be lighted from a single candle, and the life of the candle will not be shortened.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: The greatest use of a life is to spend it on something that will outlast it.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: Service to others is the rent you pay for your room here on Earth.
quotes.author: Muhammad Ali

quotes.text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
quotes.author: Aesop

quotes.text: There is no exercise better for the heart than reaching down and lifting people up.
quotes.author: John Holmes

quotes.text: What we have done for ourselves alone dies with us. What we have done for others remains.
quotes.author: Albert Pike

quotes.text: You give but little when you give of your possessions. It is when you give of yourself that you truly give.
quotes.author: Kahlil Gibran

quotes.text: Every charitable act is a stepping stone toward heaven.
quotes.author: Henry Ward Beecher

quotes.text: Giving is the master key to success in all applications of human life.
quotes.author: Bryant McGill

quotes.text: The more you give away, the more comes back to you.
quotes.author: Robin Sharma

quotes.text: Do not wait for leaders. Do it alone, person to person.
quotes.author: Mother Teresa

quotes.text: How do we change the world? One random act of kindness at a time.
quotes.author: Morgan Freeman
